behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: SEAM-MAP: module/interface seam (embedding provider output -> vector-index schema contract). MOCK-MISS: this failure appears at handoff between embedder config and index config, not inside one pure function. REAL-INFRA: behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered, but rejected because the behavior only asserts dimension compatibility, and no real network/external-service condition is required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real embedding adapter/config with index metadata in one in-process test; assert produced vector length exactly matches index dimension and that mismatch fails fast with explicit error.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is the minimum level that exercises the cross-module contract.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: SEAM-MAP: module/interface seam (query vector + vector-store client + score mapping/order). MOCK-MISS: unit-testing only a local sort function can miss score-sign/field-mapping bugs between search layer and store client. REAL-INFRA: no explicit OS/network/subprocess requirement in behavior text. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered, but rejected because the behavior is about ordering semantics at the module boundary, which can be validated with in-process wiring and controlled fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Use a test collection with known vectors and expected cosine scores, run the similarity-search module end-to-end in-process, and assert strictly non-increasing returned scores and stable tie behavior.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test crosses service/repo boundary; unit is too low, system is not strictly required by behavior text.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: SEAM-MAP: model-judgment seam (ranking quality depends on real LLM reasoning). LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model output for nuanced intent; deterministic stubs would always pass and hide failures. MULTI-STEP: this behavior targets one agentic step (re-ranker), not a full multi-agent pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow was considered, but rejected because the behavior does not require multiple agentic stages; only the re-rank decision boundary is under test.
behaviors.test_description: Run reranking against fixed candidate results and nuanced queries using the real LLM endpoint; assert that, for at least a defined subset, final order differs from raw cosine order and improves relevance judgments by labeled expectations.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Agentic is required because only real LLM behavior can reveal this failure mode.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: SEAM-MAP: module/interface seam (similarity scores + thresholding policy + response shaping). MOCK-MISS: unit-only threshold tests can miss wiring bugs where score normalization/threshold config is applied incorrectly across modules. REAL-INFRA: behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess effects. LLM-DEP: correctness here is threshold/selection logic, not LLM judgment quality. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic was considered, but rejected because the behavior text does not require model-quality evaluation; it requires enforcing an empty-set rule when closeness is below threshold.
behaviors.test_description: With controlled low-similarity fixtures, execute search + thresholding integration path and assert API-layer result set is empty (not padded) and includes expected metadata (e.g., total=0).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is the minimum level that exercises score handoff and threshold policy together.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: SEAM-MAP: multi-step orchestration seam (embedding step -> vector search -> LLM rerank -> API response). REAL-INFRA: latency failures are hidden by mocks; real service/network timing is required. MULTI-STEP: this is explicitly end-to-end across sequential model-backed steps, so it exceeds a single-step system check. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered, but rejected because behavior text explicitly requires full query-to-reranked-response path, i.e., orchestrated multi-step pipeline timing.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like E2E performance test with real embedding and reranking services over a representative query set; measure p50 wall-clock latency from HTTP request receipt to final response and fail if median exceeds 2.0s.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Workflow is the minimum that truly exercises chained real model steps and orchestration latency.
